title: Barrier-geometry sensitivity diagnostic (post-hoc, single meta)
source: DA verdict on B0022 ([review](backlog_reviews/20260526-multi-barrier-training-proposal_v1.json)) §reviewer_notes — recommended salvage variant. Originally inspired by 2026-05-26 review of Jane Street Kaggle winners (random response horizon during training).
why: B0022's instinct (barrier choice is fragile, meta should be robust to it) is sound; its mechanism (multi-barrier training) is in tension with three AFML load-bearing invariants — y is barrier-conditional, `avg_uniqueness` is barrier-conditional, threshold-0.55 is barrier-conditional. The clean version: train ONE meta on the production barrier; evaluate the SAME meta against perturbed (tp,sl,h) tuples on held-out data, WITHOUT retraining. Answers "does our chosen (tp,sl,h) sit on a stable plateau or a knife-edge?" — directly load-bearing given memory entry [barrier geometry root cause](C:/Users/LC/.claude/projects/d--PROJECTS-QuantHack/memory/project_barrier_geometry_root_cause.md) (Sharpe -0.32 → +5.18 from one barrier swap).
value_rationale: M (robustness diagnostic; no edge-change but answers a load-bearing fragility question that the project's 2026-05-25 finding flagged)
scope: new diagnostic in [pipeline/reporting.py](pipeline/reporting.py) — given a trained meta and the OOF predictions, recompute strategy metrics under K alternative barrier sets (e.g. `tp × {0.8, 1.0, 1.2}`, `h × {0.5, 1.0, 2.0}`) WITHOUT retraining. Report degradation curve in `summary.json["barrier_sensitivity"]`. Informational only; production threshold remains 0.55 against the production barrier.
